items.title: Refuses video calls or always has excuses
items.description: A real person who is interested in you will make time for a video call. Scammers avoid video because they don't look like their photos.

items.title: Wants to move off the dating app quickly
items.description: Scammers push to move to WhatsApp, Telegram, or text to avoid dating app moderation and detection systems.

items.title: Grammar/spelling inconsistent with claimed background
items.description: Claims to be American-born but writes with unusual grammar patterns common in non-native English speakers.

items.title: Messages feel scripted or generic
items.description: Responses don't reference specific things you've said, or feel like they could be sent to anyone. Scammers often copy-paste from scripts.

items.title: Messages seem AI-generated (ChatGPT-like)
items.description: Messages are overly polished, use therapeutic buzzwords ('emotional connection', 'safe space', 'navigate this journey'), or have unnaturally perfect grammar and structure. Real people text casually with typos and fragments.

items.title: Claims their camera is broken
items.description: The 'broken camera' excuse is one of the most common scammer tactics to avoid showing their real face on video.

items.title: Declares love very quickly (within days or weeks)
items.description: Love bombing is a manipulation tactic where someone overwhelms you with affection to fast-track emotional attachment and lower your defenses.

items.title: Talks about destiny, soulmates, or 'God sent you to me'
items.description: Scammers use spiritual and fate-based language to create a false sense of deep connection and make you feel the relationship is meant to be.

items.title: Gets upset or guilt-trips when questioned
items.description: When you ask reasonable questions or express doubt, they become defensive, sad, or angry to make you feel bad for being cautious.

items.title: Tries to isolate you from friends and family
items.description: Scammers don't want you talking to people who might recognize the scam. They may say your friends are jealous or don't understand your love.

items.title: Makes you feel guilty for not trusting them
items.description: Flipping the script to make your healthy skepticism seem like a character flaw is a classic manipulation technique.

items.title: Claims to be military deployed overseas
items.description: The military deployment story is extremely common in romance scams. It explains why they can't meet, can't video call, and may need money.

items.title: Profile seems too perfect or too attractive
items.description: Scammers use stolen photos of very attractive people. If their profile seems like a model or too good to be true, it probably is.

items.title: Vague or inconsistent personal details
items.description: They give conflicting information about their job, location, family, or background because they're managing multiple fake identities.

items.title: Cannot meet in person, always has excuses
items.description: Constant cancellations and excuses for not meeting are a hallmark of scammers who don't exist as they claim.

items.title: Uses stock-photo-quality profile pictures
items.description: Professional-looking photos with perfect lighting and poses may be stolen from models, influencers, or stock photo sites.

items.title: Asks for money for any reason
items.description: This is the ultimate red flag. A real romantic interest will never ask you for money, especially someone you haven't met in person.

items.title: Mentions cryptocurrency investment opportunities
items.description: This is the hallmark of a 'pig butchering' scam where they build trust then lure you into a fake crypto investment platform.

items.title: Has an 'emergency' requiring financial help
items.description: Medical emergencies, stuck at customs, legal trouble — scammers create urgent scenarios that tug at your heartstrings and demand immediate money.

items.title: Asks you to receive or forward money or packages
items.description: This makes you an unwitting money mule in a money laundering scheme, which is illegal and can result in criminal charges against you.

items.title: Requests gift cards as payment method
items.description: Gift cards are untraceable and a favorite payment method for scammers. No legitimate person or business asks for payment in gift cards.
